notes: Pages can no longer silently vanish from the editor list. The list is built from pages.json plus a server scan of your files; if that scan fails or times out (for example on a site whose data/ folder has filled up with revision snapshots) while pages.json is sparse, the list could rebuild empty and look like every page was dropped. The CMS now keeps a 'last known good' snapshot of your page list and, whenever a read it depends on fails, restores any page that was dropped (still respecting pages you've hidden, and still clearing on a genuine empty result). The snapshot is captured on every import, page save, and healthy load, so it survives refreshes — and intentional deletions are kept in sync so they don't come back. (The underlying cause on affected sites is the data/ revision build-up; the v1.14.7 api.php change stops the scanner reading data/ — deploy it to clear the root cause.) Previous (1.14.8) — Imports no longer use the Markup engine — no API tokens are spent when importing. Bringing pages into the CMS (scanning from the server or dropping a ZIP) is now a plain, fast operation: each page is read and stored as-is, with its CSS still captured so styling is preserved, and no per-page AI calls. The 'Optimize markup for editing' option and the automatic AI section-splitting have both been removed from the import flow (do that refactoring in Claude Design instead). The in-editor Markup tools are unchanged. Previous (1.14.7) — Imported pages no longer drop off — root-cause fix. The CMS writes a revision snapshot on every page save, stored in your site's data/ folder (data/rev_rev_<timestamp>.html). The page scanner was reading that folder, so those revision files showed up as 'pages' and then vanished on the next load — the editor correctly refuses to keep data files — which looked exactly like your imported pages being dropped. Now: the page scanner no longer looks inside data/ at all (it never did belong there), the page list already ignores anything under data/, and — as a safety net — if the page list ever fails to load because of a momentary network or session hiccup, the CMS keeps the pages already on screen instead of blanking the list. NOTE: the scanner change is in api.php, which is a manual deploy — deploy the updated admin/api.php to get the full root-cause fix; until then the page list still hides those files client-side, so you won't see them, and a hard-refresh ensures you're on this build. Previous (1.14.6) — Import fixes + click-anywhere rows. (1) Site Foundry no longer offers — or keeps — files that aren't real, editable pages. Data files, CMS revision snapshots (e.g. data/rev_rev_1782506528689.html), and backup files are filtered out of the scan and skipped on import (the scan now shows how many system/backup files were skipped). This is what was causing imported pages to 'disappear' after you left the editor: those entries were revision files the editor can't open, so they dropped off on the next scan — the pages list is now stable, and any such junk already saved in your pages list is cleaned up automatically on load. (2) Click anywhere on a row to act on it: the Site Foundry import scan rows now toggle their checkbox on a row click (not just the box), and the Users, Posts, and Forms lists open the editor on a row click (not just the Edit button) — controls inside a row (Edit, copy-shortcode, entry links) still do their own thing. Previous (1.14.5) — Import progress loader. Site Foundry's 'Import from Server' now shows a clear, animated progress indicator while it runs — a spinner, 'Importing page X of N' with the live step (reading / optimizing / extracting sections / saving), and a progress bar that fills as pages complete — plus a spinner on the Import button. So it's obvious the import is working and not stuck, and the loader clears the moment it finishes. Previous (1.14.4) — Import reliability fix. Site Foundry imports no longer get stuck on the 'Importing…' spinner. The per-page Markup calls (block extraction, and the optional page optimization) now have a client-side timeout, so a slow or unavailable Markup engine can never freeze an import — that page falls back to importing as-is and the run continues. The 'Optimize markup for editing' option is now OFF by default (opt-in) on both Site Foundry tabs: running the full optimization on every page made imports slow and look hung, so it's now a deliberate choice — turn it on when you want it (it processes each page through Markup, so it takes longer). Previous (1.14.3) — Editor image previews + cleaner color swatches. The 'Site Colors' captions under the editor's color pickers are gone — just the clickable swatches remain, sitting directly under each color field. And image previews in the page editor are now clear, square cards: when an image is selected, its preview at the top of the properties panel is a square box (was a short strip that squished wide logos/banners to ~50px tall), and the image picker's tiles use a square thumbnail with object-fit: contain so the full image is always visible regardless of its shape — much easier to see and pick. Previous (1.14.2) — Site color palette in the editor. Wherever you set a color in the page editor — text color, background, and button/link hover colors — a 'site colors' palette now appears beneath the picker: the colors actually used on the page, listed most-used to least-used, with your brand colors always included. Click a swatch to apply it instantly, so it's easy to stay on-palette without copying hex codes. (The palette reads the live page's rendered colors and refreshes when you open a different page.) Previous (1.14.1) — Editor freshness + SEO accuracy + Design polish. (1) The page editor now always opens the freshest published version of a page — it cache-busts the file when loading, so a stale browser-cached copy can no longer show up after you publish and then refresh the CMS. (2) The SEO analysis is fairer and more technical, closer to a Lighthouse audit. Keyword matching is no longer punctuation-literal: a focus keyword like 'doors vermillion sd' now correctly matches a title of 'Doors, Vermillion, SD' (and partial word matches count, so it isn't overly strict about exact phrasing). The checklist adds technical health checks read from the live page — descriptive (non-placeholder) title, indexable (not noindex), canonical available, exactly one H1, all images have alt text, and enough content — alongside the keyword checks. Generated SEO now produces a clean primary keyword (lowercase, no punctuation) whose words appear naturally in the title and description. (3) The Design tab's Primary and Secondary brand-color pickers are now proper squares instead of wide, short boxes. (4) Client-facing wording no longer mentions 'Claude' or an API key: Site Foundry shows a plain 'Markup isn't enabled yet' note instead of 'No Claude API key — add it in Settings', and the editor's status and error messages use 'Markup'. Previous (1.14.0) — Automatic markup optimization in Site Foundry. When you bring a site into the CMS — either by scanning the files on your server or by dropping a ZIP — each page's markup can now be optimized for the editor: semantic landmarks (header / nav / main / footer), named sections with clear ids, the standard FAQ/accordion structure, proper headings (one H1 per page), real image tags with alt text, a single place for CSS, brand-color variables, and clean file naming (index.html for the homepage and a single .html extension for the rest). A new 'Optimize markup for editing' toggle (on by default, shown when Markup is available) controls it on both Site Foundry tabs. Your design is never changed: every optimized page is run through a fidelity check first — if its visible text or its stylesheet didn't survive intact, the original page is kept untouched — and the page's scripts are preserved verbatim across the round trip. When scanning from the server, the optimized page is written back to the server file (and pushed to GitHub when connected) so the editor opens the improved version; pages too large to optimize safely, or that don't pass the check, are imported as-is. Previous (1.13.8) — Faithful mobile/tablet preview + reachable controls. The Tablet and Mobile previews now emulate a real device like the browser's inspector: the page renders at the device's true CSS width (mobile 390×844, tablet 768×1024) so its own responsive CSS reflows exactly as on the device, and the whole frame scales down to fit the editor — much closer to what you see on your phone. Editing handles now stay grabbable even when an element runs off the edge of a narrow preview: the resize handles clamp to the visible edge instead of disappearing off-screen (and the move/duplicate/delete + size controls in the right panel are always available). Previous (1.13.7) — Tidy-up: the Hover controls in the editor are now labeled just 'Background' and 'Text' (they already sit under the 'Hover' heading, so the repeated word is gone). Previous (1.13.6) — Editor breathing room + privacy-page layout. Generated pages (privacy policy, new pages) now place the content in a centered column with real side margins whose layout is locked with !important so the site's own CSS can't stretch it edge-to-edge, and the real header and footer are kept inside their original wrapper (so their styling holds) — the content between them is swapped rather than rebuilt. The main left sidebar now auto-collapses when you enter the page editor (more room for the preview) and expands again elsewhere; you can still toggle it any time with the button by the logo. The desktop preview is now resizable — grab the orange handle at its bottom-right corner and drag to set any size (switching device resets it). Previous (1.13.5) — Font picker upgrades for per-page heading styles. Each font in the dropdown now renders in its own typeface (the curated fonts load into the admin so you can see them). Added a 'More fonts…' option that opens a searchable picker for the entire Google Fonts library — type to filter, each result previews in its font, and you can apply any font by exact name even if it's not in the list (the browse list is fetched live, with the built-in set as a fallback). Picked fonts are added to the dropdown and load on the page automatically. Previous (1.13.4) — Editor polish + fixes. The page editor's properties panel no longer has an 'Apply Changes' button — every edit (text, color, size, weight, alignment, spacing, links, anchors, image source/alt) now applies automatically as you make it. Text alignment now applies reliably (written with !important so a theme's own rules can't override it). Buttons and links gain Hover controls — set a hover background and text color, saved as a per-element :hover rule in the page. Per-page heading styles now cover H1–H6 (was H1–H3) and add a Google Fonts picker; the chosen fonts are loaded on the page automatically. New pages and the privacy-policy page now sit in a centered content section with real side margins and room below the header (no more overlap), while still pulling in the site's real header and footer. Fixed the Restore button in Site Foundry → Manage Pages (it wasn't firing due to a quote-escaping bug) and removed the now-redundant remove buttons there. Reworded client-facing messages so they no longer reference internal system files. Previous (1.13.3) — Subtle version stamp in the bottom-left of the sidebar (small gray monospace, e.g. 'v1.13.3') so you can see which build is loaded at a glance — it reads the live CMS_VERSION and hides when the sidebar is collapsed. Previous (1.13.2) — New + generated pages now inherit the site header/footer. The privacy-policy generator and the New Page action used to emit a standalone document with no header/nav/footer; they now clone a real page (its head/CSS + header + nav + footer) and swap in the new content, so created pages match the rest of the site (falls back to a standalone doc only if there's no donor page). Resize anchoring fixed properly: a normal drag now keeps the OPPOSITE edge/corner fixed (drag the bottom-right and the left/top stay put) no matter how the element is centered — it probes how margin maps to on-screen position, then measures and corrects so the anchor holds even for flex- or text-align-centered elements (the old margin-pin didn't work on those, so it looked symmetric). Hold Cmd (Mac) / Ctrl (PC) for symmetric resize from the center. Site Foundry → Manage Pages: removed the redundant 'Remove from CMS' buttons (removal lives in the editor's gear → Page Settings); the page list + Restore for hidden pages remain. The sidebar 'Pages' count (and dashboard stat) now update immediately whenever a page is removed, deleted, restored, or added. Design tab: brand colors are now Primary + Secondary, bound to the --brand-color-primary / --brand-color-secondary CSS variables. They auto-fill from the site's CSS (and empty Contact/Social fields auto-fill from the site's links); 'Apply colors to all pages' injects a managed :root override into every page so the variables update site-wide. Previous (1.13.1) — Fixes. Remove-from-CMS (Site Foundry → Manage Pages, and the Page Settings gear): now matches the page by its derived id (pageIdFromPath normalizes the path deterministically) plus a normalized full-path backstop, instead of brittle filename/path string compares — so removal sticks for subdirectory pages and survives path-format drift (case, leading ./ or /). It no longer wrongly hides a different page that happens to share a filename (e.g. services/plumbing.html vs plumbing.html), and loadPages now makes sure site.json is loaded before filtering so a removed page can't reappear when the editor opens before the Design tab. Div/container resize handles: each drag now sets an absolute size from a fresh snapshot and the element no longer re-centers between drags — margin-left/top are pinned on the active axis so an auto-centered element (margin:0 auto) stays put. Default drag anchors the opposite edge/corner; hold Cmd (Mac) / Ctrl (PC) to resize symmetrically from the center (opposite edge moves equally), like Photoshop. Previous (1.13.0) — Password-protect pages (PHP session gate). Page Settings now has a 'Password-protect this page' toggle + password field. When enabled, api.php stores a bcrypt hash of the password in admin/protect.secret.php (a PHP file, never served as source), (re)writes a public _fourge_gate.php, and maintains a managed RewriteRule block in the root .htaccess so the page routes through the gate. Visitors get a branded password screen; on the correct password the gate sets a PHP session cookie and serves the page (path-traversal-safe, only pages in the protect list are servable, wrong attempts are slowed and the hash never reaches the browser). Protected pages show a lock icon in the editor's page list. NOTE: requires the updated api.php deployed by hand (api.php is always a manual deploy); the gate file + .htaccess rules are written automatically the first time you protect a page. Previous (1.12.0) — Responsive editing (Elementor-style funnel) + reverse columns. The Desktop/Tablet/Mobile toggle in the editor now scopes your edits: switch to Tablet or Mobile and any style change (font size, color, weight, alignment, spacing, radius, background, width/height — including drag-resize) is written as a per-device media-query rule instead of an inline style, keyed to a stable per-element class. Values funnel down the cascade — Desktop applies everywhere, Tablet overrides tablet+mobile (≤1024px), Mobile overrides mobile (≤600px) — so you only override what differs. The properties panel shows a banner telling you which device you're editing. Flex containers get a Direction control (row / row-reverse / column / column-reverse); set it per device to reverse or stack columns on phones. Overrides are saved into a managed <style id=fourge-responsive> block in the page and the element classes, so they reconstruct correctly when you reopen the page (and a new override never wipes existing ones). Previous (1.11.0) — Collapsible side menus + drag-to-resize containers. The Desktop/Tablet/Mobile toggle in the editor now scopes your edits: switch to Tablet or Mobile and any style change (font size, color, weight, alignment, spacing, radius, background, width/height — including drag-resize) is written as a per-device media-query rule instead of an inline style, keyed to a stable per-element class. Values funnel down the cascade — Desktop applies everywhere, Tablet overrides tablet+mobile (≤1024px), Mobile overrides mobile (≤600px) — so you only override what differs. The properties panel shows a banner telling you which device you're editing. Flex containers get a Direction control (row / row-reverse / column / column-reverse); set it per device to reverse or stack columns on phones. Overrides are saved into a managed <style id=fourge-responsive> block in the page and the element classes, so they reconstruct correctly when you reopen the page (and a new override never wipes existing ones). Previous (1.11.0) — Collapsible side menus + drag-to-resize containers. Both side menus now collapse: a toggle by the logo collapses the main sidebar, and a toggle in the editor toolbar collapses the page list in the page editor — each slides to zero width and the choice persists per browser. Drag-to-resize: selecting a container that holds text (or an image) in the visual editor now shows eight resize handles (four corners + four edge midpoints). Drag any handle to resize — corners resize both dimensions, edges resize one; dragging the left/top edges keeps the opposite edge anchored (margin compensation). The new width/height (and any margin) are written as inline styles on the element, so the change is saved accurately into the page's HTML. A resize is one undo step. Previous (1.10.0) — Editor + site polish from the testing meeting. Links: the properties panel now has an 'Open in a new tab' checkbox for any link (sets target=_blank + rel=noopener noreferrer) and clears it cleanly when unchecked. Anchors: every element (especially sections and columns) now has an editable 'Anchor ID' field, so you can link to a spot on the page with #id — the id is sanitized to a valid anchor as you type, and the panel shows the exact #link to use. Favicon: Design → Design now has a Favicon uploader (square PNG/SVG/ICO) with a live preview; 'Apply to all pages' injects a managed <link rel=icon> (+ apple-touch-icon) into every page and Remove clears it. Per-page heading styles: Page Settings now has H1/H2/H3 overrides (size, weight, alignment, color) that compile to scoped, !important CSS injected only into that page on Save, so a page can override the site design without touching global styles. Go Live tool: Settings now has a 'Find & Replace URL' card (Architect only) to swap a staging/preview domain for the production domain across all pages, posts, sitemap.xml and robots.txt — with a non-destructive Preview (per-file occurrence counts) before applying, and it also fixes the website URL in site.json. Renamed the last visible 'SITE FACTORY' label to 'SITE FOUNDRY'. Previous (1.9.0) — Page management: each page in the editor now has a gear → Page Settings (Custom Page CSS + Custom Page JavaScript injected into that page only on save, Draft toggle that noindexes + drops the page from the sitemap, Delete page, and Remove-from-CMS). Targeted-landing pages now show a red dot in the page list. Fixed Remove-from-CMS not sticking (the hidden-page match now checks path OR file OR id). Polish: the SEO Generate buttons now read 'Generate' / 'Generate All' (dropped 'with AI') with a clean Font Awesome icon instead of the sparkle emoji — no emojis in the UI. Renamed the Site Factory module to Site Foundry throughout. SEO module rebuilt (Rank Math-style): AI generation now reads each page's REAL content (fixes the 'Page Not Found' metadata bug where it read empty CMS blocks on scanned pages) and writes context-aware, business/location-specific titles + descriptions. Per-page SEO now includes focus keyword with a live on-page analysis checklist + score, canonical URL, robots index/noindex + follow/nofollow, Open Graph + Twitter card fields, a Google snippet preview, and a per-page schema type. New 'Apply to all pages' writes managed meta tags + JSON-LD (site-wide LocalBusiness/Organization + per-page schema) into every page file and regenerates sitemap.xml + robots.txt. Sitemap generator and robots.txt editor added. (Redirections + a 404 monitor are the next SEO addition.) Also fixed the editor toolbar: the Visual/Code toggle is centered and the device buttons no longer break when returning from Code view. Code editor for the page editor: a Visual ⇄ Code toggle in the editor toolbar opens a VS Code-style source view (CodeMirror, syntax-highlighted HTML + inline CSS/JS, dark theme, line numbers, auto-close tags, Ctrl/Cmd+S to save). Switching back to Visual ingests any hand-edits so they stay editable visually; saving in Code mode writes the source directly. Fixed a media-gallery bug where, on a filtered view (e.g. Videos), clicking a tile opened/copied the item at that slot in the unfiltered 'All' list instead of the one you clicked. Editor preview now fills the canvas: the desktop preview is the largest 16:9 box that fits (rendering at a real desktop width — at least 1440, larger on big monitors) plus a Fill-screen button (native fullscreen). Fixed self profile editing — the signed-in user's id and email are now stored in the session so the Account modal populates and saves (was 'Please sign in again'). Added custom code injection points in Analytics: Functional/Analytics/Marketing each with a header and a body field, injected into <head> and before </body> via the existing Apply-to-All-Pages sweep. Account/Settings cleanup: removed the Updates card and the Mailgun card from Settings (Mailgun is now configured server-side in config.secret.php so the API key never passes through the browser); removed the top-right notification bell; the top-right avatar is now a profile menu (Account / Change password / Sign out) and the change-password + sign-out buttons moved out of the bottom-left into it; signed-in users can now edit their own profile (name + email) via the new Account modal. ADAptify panel isolation fix: the widget is now appended to <html> (a sibling of <body>) and every visitor effect is scoped to <body>, so the panel itself can never be recolored, filtered, zoomed, or resized by the page — verified the menu stays readable under High Contrast/Invert. ADAptify full option set: the widget now offers Readable Font, Highlight Titles/Links, Text Magnifier, Content Scaling + Font Size + Line Height + Letter Spacing steppers (±10% per click), text alignment, Dark/Light/High contrast, Monochrome, Invert, High/Low saturation, custom text/title/background colors, Mute Sounds, Hide Images, Reading Guide, Reading Mask, Stop Animations, Highlight on Hover/Click, and Large Cursor — grouped into Content/Color/Navigation sections, all persisted per visitor. Updates are now ALWAYS automatic: the manual update card is removed and every site installs the latest engine at login, so no one can run a stale build (re-introduce a gate before a major release if needed). Security: enforce HTTPS for credentials. A new root .htaccess redirects all HTTP traffic to HTTPS site-wide (proxy-aware to avoid redirect loops), and api.php now refuses sign-in / password / secret actions over plain HTTP as a server-side backstop (localhost exempt; toggle with 'require_https' in config.secret.php). Passwords were already hashed at rest with Argon2id/bcrypt; this closes the in-transit gap. Hotfix (1.4.1): neutralized a literal closing-script sequence in an ADAptify comment that truncated admin/index.html and broke login. ADAptify — a site-wide accessibility widget (contrast, text size, grayscale, invert, readable font, highlight links, big cursor, pause motion) with its own CMS tab. Enable/disable, placement, and brand colors are stored server-side in data/adaptify.json and apply to every page instantly. One-click install drops the widget script across all pages. Plus the 1.3.0 work: server-level page list, automatic server connection, server-side settings, Revisions ledger, faithful HTML import, WAF-proof multipart save, square media picker + 16:9 desktop editor, FAQ/accordion editing, and Privacy Policy generator.
apiNote: admin/api.php holds this site's secrets (API token) and is never auto-updated. If a release changes api.php, update it by hand. 1.14.7 adds 'data' to the page scanner's SKIP_DIRS so it no longer lists the CMS's own data/ folder (pages.json, site.json, and per-save revision snapshots data/rev_rev_*.html) as importable pages — deploy the updated api.php so revision files never reach the scan; until then the admin filters them client-side. 1.13.0 adds the 'set_page_password' action (per-page password protection): deploy the updated api.php by hand for page protection to work — the gate file (_fourge_gate.php) and .htaccess rules are written automatically when you first protect a page; the bcrypt hashes live in admin/protect.secret.php (never served as source). 1.4.2 adds HTTPS enforcement to api.php (the credential-action backstop) — deploy the updated api.php and the new root .htaccess by hand to get the full effect; the redirect and guard only protect a site once those two files are in place. 1.4.0 adds 'adaptify.js' to api.php's repo-fetch allow-list — only needed for installing the widget from a PRIVATE repo.
